name: Божественная Душа
description: Sometimes the spark of magic that fuels a sorcerer comes from a divine source that glimmers within the soul. Having such a blessed soul is a sign that your innate magic might come from a distant but powerful familial connection to a divine being. Perhaps your ancestor was an angel, transformed into a mortal and sent to fight in a god's name. Or your birth might align with an ancient prophecy, marking you as a servant of the gods or a chosen vessel of divine magic. A Divine Soul, with a natural magnetism, is seen as a threat by some religious hierarchies. As an outsider who commands sacred power, a Divine Soul can undermine an existing order by claiming a direct tie to the divine. In some cultures, only those who can claim the power of a Divine Soul may command religious power. In these lands, ecclesiastical positions are dominated by a few bloodlines and preserved over generations.
shortDescription: Channel Divine Power Through Your Soul
note: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature.

features.name: Divine Magic
features.description: Your link to the divine allows you to learn spells from the cleric class. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn or replace a sorcerer cantrip or a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose the new spell from the {@filter Cleric spell list} or the {@filter Sorcerer spell list}. You must otherwise obey all the restrictions for selecting the spell, and it becomes a sorcerer spell for you. In addition, choose an affinity for the source of your divine power: good, evil, law, chaos, or neutrality. You learn an additional spell based on that affinity, as shown below. It is a sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against your number of sorcerer spells known. If you later replace this spell, you must replace it with a spell from the {@filter Cleric spell list}.

features.name: Favored by the Gods
features.description: Divine power guards your destiny. If you fail a saving throw or miss with an attack roll, you can roll {@dice 2d4} and add it to the total, possibly changing the outcome. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Empowered Healing
features.description: The divine energy coursing through you can empower healing spells. Whenever you or an ally within 5 feet of you rolls dice to determine the number of hit points a spell restores, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll any number of those dice once, provided you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. You can use this feature only once per turn.

features.name: Otherworldly Wings
features.description:
You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to manifest a pair of spectral wings from your back. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed of 30 feet. The wings last until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you die, or you dismiss them as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

The affinity you chose for your Divine Magic feature determines the appearance of the spectral wings: eagle wings for good or law, bat wings for evil or chaos, and dragonfly wings for neutrality.

features.name: Unearthly Recovery
features.description:
You gain the ability to overcome grievous injuries. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} when you have fewer than half of your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} remaining, you can regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Hit Point maximum.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
